'''''UFC 316: Dvalishvili vs. O'Malley 2''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on June 7, 2025, at the [[Prudential Center]], in [[Newark, New Jersey]], United States.<ref name="Newark">{{Cite web|url=https://www.ufc.com/news/dana-white-announces-two-title-fights-ufc-316-newark-dvalishvili-omalley-pena-harrison|title=Dana White announces two title fights for UFC 316 in Newark|publisher=ufc.com|author=Kevin Schuster|date=2025-04-01|access-date=2025-04-01}}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's 11th visit to Newark and first since [[UFC 302]] in June 2024.<ref name="Newark"/>

A [[UFC Bantamweight Championship]] rematch between current champion [[Merab Dvalishvili]] and former champion [[Sean O'Malley (fighter)|Sean O'Malley]] headlined this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/04/ufc-316-merab-dvalishvili-vs-sean-omalley-rematch-booked|title=Merab Dvalishvili vs. Sean O'Malley title rematch headlines UFC 316 in Newark|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2025-04-01|access-date=2025-04-01}}</ref> The pairing previously met in September 2024 at [[UFC 306]] in which Dvalishvili captured the title by unanimous decision.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2024/09/15/ufc-306-merab-dvalishvilis-machine-like-wrestling-dethrones-sean-omalley/|title=UFC 306: Merab Dvalishvili’s machine-like wrestling dethrones Sean O’Malley|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Staff|date=2024-09-14|access-date=2024-09-14}}</ref>

A [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship]] bout between current two-time champion (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Rousey vs. Team Tate]] bantamweight winner) [[Julianna Peña]] and the [[Judo at the 2012 Summer Olympics – Women's 78 kg|2012]] and [[Judo at the 2016 Summer Olympics – Women's 78 kg|2016 Olympic gold medalist in judo]] (also two-time [[Professional Fighters League|PFL]] women's lightweight tournament champion) [[Kayla Harrison]] co-headlined this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/brianmazique/2025/02/25/ufc-316-long-awaited-championship-fight-set-for-newark-ppv/|title=UFC 316: long-awaited championship fight set for Newark PPV|publisher=forbes.com|author=Brian Mazique|date=2025-02-25|access-date=2025-02-25}}</ref>

A bantamweight bout between former title challenger [[Marlon Vera]] and [[Mario Bautista]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Date change! The fight between Marlon "Chito" Vera and Mario Bautista has been rescheduled for June 7|url=https://orbitadeportiva.net/2025/03/26/cambio-de-fecha-el-combate-entre-marlon-chito-vera-y-mario-bautista-se-reprogramaria-para-el-7-de-junio/|date=2025-03-26|access-date=2025-03-26|publisher=orbitadeportiva.net|author=CoelloC|archive-date=2025-03-26|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250326171259/https://orbitadeportiva.net/2025/03/26/cambio-de-fecha-el-combate-entre-marlon-chito-vera-y-mario-bautista-se-reprogramaria-para-el-7-de-junio/|url-status=dead}} {{in lang|es}}</ref> They were previously expected to meet at [[UFC Fight Night: Sandhagen vs. Figueiredo]] in May, but the pairing was moved to this event for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://empiresportsmedia.com/mma/ufc-des-moines-adding-marlon-chito-vera-mario-bautista-report/|title=UFC Des Moines adding Marlon "Chito" Vera – Mario Bautista (Report)|publisher=empiresportsmedia.com|author=Daniel Cunningham|date=2025-02-26|access-date=2025-03-26}}</ref> In turn, Vera pulled out in mid May for undisclosed reasons and was replaced by promotional newcomer and former [[List of Bellator MMA champions#Bellator Bantamweight World Championship|Bellator Bantamweight World Champion]] [[Patchy Mix]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/5/14/24430102/chito-vera-out-of-ufc-316-bout-with-mario-bautista|title=Chito Vera out of UFC 316 bout with Mario Bautista|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2025-05-14|access-date=2025-05-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.lowkickmma.com/patchy-mix-fight-with-mario-bautista-at-ufc-316/|title=Report – Patchy Mix set to replace Chito Vera, make Octagon bow in fight with Mario Bautista at UFC 316|publisher=lowkickmma.com|author=Ross Markey|date=2025-05-14|access-date=2025-05-14}}</ref>

A flyweight bout between [[Bruno Gustavo da Silva]] and [[Joshua Van]] took place at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Joshua-Van-vs-Bruno-Silva-Rebooked-for-UFC-316-196501|title=Joshua Van vs. Bruno Silva Rebooked for UFC 316|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Tristen Critchfield|date=2025-03-26|access-date=2025-03-26}}</ref> They were previously scheduled to compete at [[UFC 313]] in March, but Silva had to withdraw due to an injury.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/2/17/24367343/bruno-silva-out-of-ufc-313-rei-tsuruya-steps-in-against-joshua-van|title=Bruno Silva out of UFC 313, Rei Tsuruya steps in against Joshua Van|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Jed Meshew|date=2025-02-17|access-date=2025-02-17}}</ref>

[[Serghei Spivac]] and [[Shamil Gaziev]] were expected to meet in a heavyweight bout at [[UFC Fight Night: Burns vs. Morales]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://allboxing.ru/news/20250228-0920/sergey-spivak-i-shamil-gaziev-mogut-provesti-boy-17-maya|title=Sergey Spivak and Shamil Gaziev may fight on May 17|publisher=allboxing.ru|author=Kirill Ivanov|date=2025-02-28|access-date=2025-02-28}} {{in lang|ru}}</ref> However, the bout was moved to this event due to undisclosed reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://athlonsports.com/mma/ufc-serghei-spivac-vs-shamil-gaziev-fight-announced-ufc-316|title=UFC 316 fight card levels up – heavyweights join the main card|publisher=athlonsports.com|author=Prince Horton|date=2025-04-01|access-date=2025-04-01}}</ref> In turn, Gaziev pulled out due to undisclosed reasons and was replaced by former [[Legacy Fighting Alliance#LFA Heavyweight Championship|LFA Heavyweight Champion]] [[Waldo Cortes-Acosta]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://vringe.com/mma/news/188256-ne-gaziev-u-spivaka-novyy-sopernik-po-ufc-316.htm|title=Not Gaziev. Spivak has a new opponent at UFC 316|publisher=vringe.com|author=Staff|date=2025-04-24|access-date=2025-04-24}} {{in lang|ru}}</ref>

A light heavyweight bout between [[Johnny Walker (fighter)|Johnny Walker]] and [[Azamat Murzakanov]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2025/04/01/dvalishvili-vs-omalley-2-pena-vs-harrison-official-for-ufc-316/|title=Dvalishvili vs. O’Malley 2, Peña vs. ←Harrison official for UFC 316|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Eddie Law|date=2025-04-01|access-date=2025-04-01}}</ref> They were originally scheduled for UFC Fight Night: Burns vs. Morales but were moved to this event for unknown reasons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/03/ufc-news-johnny-walker-vs-azamat-murzakanov|title=UFC books Johnny Walker vs. Azamat Murzakanov|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2025-03-12|access-date=2025-03-13}}</ref> However, Walker pulled out in early May due to injury and was replaced by Brendson Ribeiro.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.superlutas.com.br/noticias/316865/brendson-ribeiro-substitui-johnny-walker-ufc-316/|title=Brendson Ribeiro replaces Johnny Walker and faces ranked opponent at UFC 316|publisher=superlutas.com.br|author=Léo Guimarães|date=2025-05-06|access-date=2025-05-07}} {{in lang|pt-br}}</ref>

Former interim [[UFC Middleweight Championship]] challenger (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Jones vs. Team Sonnen]] middleweight winner) [[Kelvin Gastelum]] and [[Joe Pyfer]] fought in a middleweight bout at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/4/7/24403368/kelvin-gastelum-vs-joe-pyfer-rebooked-for-ufc-316|title=Kelvin Gastelum vs. Joe Pyfer rebooked for UFC 316|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2025-04-07|access-date=2025-04-07}}</ref> They were previously scheduled to meet at [[UFC on ESPN: Moreno vs. Erceg]] in March, but the fight was cancelled on the day of the event as Pyfer had to withdraw due to an illness.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/03/ufc-mexico-joe-pyfer-vs-kelvin-gastelum|title=UFC on ESPN 64 loses Joe Pyfer vs. Kelvin Gastelum hours before Mexico event|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-03-29|access-date=2025-03-29}}</ref>

[[Uroš Medić]] was expected to meet [[Khaos Williams]] in a welterweight bout on the preliminary card.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/5/6/24421388/khaos-williams-fights-uros-medic-at-ufc-316|title=Khaos Williams fights Uros Medic at UFC 316|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Jed Meshew|date=2025-05-06|access-date=2025-05-06}}</ref> However, Medić pulled out in late May due to sinusitis and was replaced by promotional newcomer Albert Tadevosyan.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sd.rs/sport/ostali-sportovi/nista-od-borbe-srbina-u-ufc-u-doktor-morao-da-se-povuce-evo-i-iz-kog-razloga-2025-05-29|title=No Serbian fight in the UFC: "The Doctor" had to withdraw, and here's why|publisher=sd.rs|author=Nenad Ranković|date=2025-05-29|access-date=2025-05-29}} {{in lang|sr}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/05/29/ufc-316-khaos-williams-vs-albert-tadevosyan-new-matchup-uros-medic-out/83933429007/|title=UFC 316: Khaos Williams gets opponent change, faces newcomer Albert Tadevosyan|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Matthew Wells and Nolan King|date=2025-05-29|access-date=2025-05-29}}</ref> In turn, Tadevosyan did not pass his medical tests, so Williams instead faced another newcomer in Andreas Gustafsson, who was originally scheduled to compete at [[UFC on ESPN: Gamrot vs. Klein]] one week before.<ref name="Andreas">{{Cite web|url=https://www.bodyslam.net/2025/05/30/ufc-316-card-updates-fights-added-and-changed/|title=UFC 316 Card Updates, Fights Added And Changed|publisher=bodyslam.net|author=Brady Alexander|date=2025-05-30|access-date=2025-05-30}}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, former [[Konfrontacja Sztuk Walki#Women's flyweight championship|KSW Women's Flyweight Champion]] [[Ariane da Silva]] weighed in at 132 pounds, six pounds over the flyweight non-title fight limit. Her bout with [[Wang Cong]] proceeded at catchweight and she was fined 25 percent of her purse which went to Wang.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bleacherreport.com/articles/25203190-ufc-316-live-winners-and-losers-results|title=UFC 316 Live Winners and Losers, Results|publisher=bleacherreport.com|author=Lyle Fitzsimmons|date=2025-06-07|access-date=2025-06-07}}</ref>

During the event's broadcast, two-time [[List of UFC Champions#Tournament Winners|UFC Heavyweight Tournament winner]] ([[UFC 14]] and [[UFC 15]]) [[Mark Kerr (fighter)|Mark Kerr]] was announced as the next "pioneer wing" and final [[UFC Hall of Fame]] inductee of this year during International Fight Week festivities in Las Vegas this June.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2025/06/07/mark-kerr-headed-to-ufc-hall-of-fame-class-of-2025/|title=Mark Kerr headed to UFC Hall of Fame, Class of 2025|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Jay Anderson|date=2025-06-07|access-date=2025-06-07}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2025/6/8/24444941/official-bonus-winners-ufc-316-results-espn-ppv-newark-dvalishvili-harrison-holland-yoo|title=Bonuses! Kevin Holland banks $50K for 10th-career post-fight award - UFC 316|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2025-06-08|access-date=2025-06-08}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night:''' No bonus awarded.
*'''Performance of the Night: Merab Dvalishvili''', '''Kayla Harrison''', '''Kevin Holland''', and '''Yoo Joo-sang'''
